prompts.label: SELECT and WHERE basics
prompts.text:
I'm learning SQL. Create a teaching example with a users table.

- CREATE TABLE users (id, name, email, created_at)
- INSERT 5 sample rows
- SELECT * and SELECT with WHERE age or name filter

Use SQLite-compatible syntax and explain each clause.

prompts.label: ORDER BY and LIMIT
prompts.text:
Extend a users table lesson with sorting and pagination.

- ORDER BY created_at DESC
- LIMIT 3 OFFSET 0
- Example: page 2 with OFFSET 3 LIMIT 3

Show expected result order in comments.

prompts.label: INNER JOIN orders and users
prompts.text:
Teach INNER JOIN for beginners.

- tables: users and orders (user_id foreign key)
- Sample data and query listing order id, user name, amount
- Diagram in ASCII or text: how JOIN matches rows

Add one example where a user has zero orders (and whether they appear).

prompts.label: LEFT JOIN all users with orders
prompts.text:
Explain LEFT JOIN vs INNER JOIN with the same users/orders schema.

- Query that lists every user and order amount or NULL
- When to use LEFT JOIN in real apps (2 examples)

Keep SQLite syntax.

prompts.label: COUNT, SUM, GROUP BY
prompts.text:
Teach aggregation functions for a beginner.

- COUNT(*) orders per user_id with GROUP BY
- HAVING to filter groups with count > 1
- SUM(amount) per user

Provide sample tables and full queries.

prompts.label: INSERT and UPDATE safely
prompts.text:
Show INSERT and UPDATE with safety tips.

- INSERT one row with explicit columns
- UPDATE email WHERE id = 1
- Warn about UPDATE without WHERE with a scary example comment

Mention transactions in one paragraph (BEGIN/COMMIT).

prompts.label: DELETE and soft delete pattern
prompts.text:
Explain DELETE and the soft-delete pattern.

- Hard DELETE with WHERE
- Alternative: deleted_at TIMESTAMP NULL
- SELECT that hides soft-deleted rows

When do teams prefer soft delete? Short answer.

prompts.label: Primary and foreign keys
prompts.text:
Design a tiny blog schema for learning keys.

- posts (id PK, title, body)
- comments (id PK, post_id FK, text)
- CREATE TABLE statements with constraints
- One invalid INSERT that fails FK — explain error

SQLite syntax.

prompts.label: Indexes explained simply
prompts.text:
Explain database indexes for a beginner (no deep internals).

- Analogy: book index vs reading every page
- CREATE INDEX on users(email)
- When indexes help and when too many hurt

Use a small example query that benefits from an index.

prompts.label: Subquery in WHERE
prompts.text:
Introduce subqueries with a practical example.

- Find users who have at least one order using IN (SELECT user_id FROM orders)
- Rewrite with EXISTS briefly
- Which style is often clearer for beginners?

Include CREATE + INSERT sample data.

prompts.label: HAVING vs WHERE with aggregates
prompts.text:
I mix WHERE and HAVING incorrectly when filtering grouped results.
Use a orders table example: total spend per customer, show only customers over $500.
Explain execution order conceptually (FROM → WHERE → GROUP BY → HAVING).
Give a wrong query and the corrected one side by side.
Add a one-sentence note about alias limitations in HAVING for beginners.

prompts.label: COALESCE and NULLIF patterns
prompts.text:
Teach COALESCE to provide defaults and NULLIF to turn sentinel values into NULL.
Example: display display_name or username or email.
Show NULLIF for empty string vs real null cleanup before unique constraints.
Contrast with CASE verbosity.
Include a pitfall about COALESCE type widening in some engines (high level).

prompts.label: Common Table Expressions readability
prompts.text:
Rewrite a gnarly nested subquery using WITH named CTEs for monthly active users.
Explain how CTEs aid readability, not always performance.
Show chaining two CTEs where the second depends on the first.
Mention recursive CTEs only as a teaser with a one-line example idea.
Keep dialect SQL mostly ANSI-like.

prompts.label: Window functions ROW_NUMBER primer
prompts.text:
Introduce ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY user_id ORDER BY created_at DESC) to pick latest row per group.
Compare with GROUP BY max patterns.
Explain why DISTINCT ON (Postgres) is different bonus note.
Provide a dedup problem statement beginners recognize.
Warn about requiring ORDER BY for deterministic ranking.

prompts.label: CASE expressions in SELECT
prompts.text:
Use CASE to bucket ages or order statuses into human labels inside SQL.
Show simple searched CASE vs expression CASE.
Explain short-circuit evaluation expectations cautiously per engine.
Provide an example joining to a reference table alternative.
End with style tips for readable CASE formatting.

prompts.label: Indexes and WHERE column order
prompts.text:
Explain composite index (a,b) vs (b,a) with a query that can use one but not the other.
Teach covering indexes conceptually without deep internals.
Relate to EXPLAIN as a black-box flashlight—what to look for first.
List a common OR condition pitfall.
Encourage measuring on realistic row counts.

prompts.label: Soft delete with partial unique index idea
prompts.text:
Model soft deletes with deleted_at timestamps.
Explain why unique emails need special handling if multiple inactive rows exist.
Outline partial unique indexes where supported as a solution sketch.
Contrast with separate archive table trade-off.
Keep guidance conceptual for beginners migrating from hard DELETE.

prompts.label: Transactions ACID story with transfer
prompts.text:
Tell the classic debit/credit money transfer story needing transactions.
Show BEGIN; two updates; COMMIT; and ROLLBACK on error pseudo-code.
Explain isolation levels only as a vocabulary teaser.
Relate to ORMs wrapping units of work.
Note deadlock possibility at a high level.

prompts.label: Views for repeated complex joins
prompts.text:
Create a view customer_order_summary joining customers, orders, aggregates.
Discuss permissions and hiding column complexity for reporting users.
Note materialized views as a performance tool name-drop.
Warn that views are not a substitute for proper indexing.
Give when to prefer a view vs a stored procedure light mention.

prompts.label: Anti-join for missing records
prompts.text:
Find users who have never placed an order using LEFT JOIN ... WHERE order.id IS NULL.
Show equivalent NOT EXISTS pattern and why it reads clearer to some teams.
Explain NULL handling surprises in OUTER JOIN filters.
Provide a second example: products never sold.
Finish with a caution about duplicates inflating LEFT JOIN rows.
